rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DEIRDRE RAND PHD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Modify Protective Order And Request For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday March 10, 2015, Line 7, Motion To Modify Protective Order And Request For Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion to modify the protective order is GRANTED. The respondent's request for sanctions is GRANTED IN PART, DENIED IN PART. The request for sanctions stemming from the 2013 motion is DENIED. The request for sanctions related to the current motion is GRANTED in the amount of $10,000, payable within 14 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: DIANE RICHARDS VS. BUREAU OF AUTOMOTIVE REPAIR
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 10, 2015, Line 2, RESPONDENT BUREAU OF AUTOMOTIVE REPAIR DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION. The court treats this demurrer as a motion for judgment on the pleadings and grants the motion without leave to amend. See CCP 438(c)(3)(B)(ii). The action is time barred under Gov't Code sec. 11523 and petitioner fails to explain how it can amend the petition to cure the defect. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Strike
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday March 10, 2015, Line 2, PETITIONER DAVID KENT, DARCIE KENT'S Motion To Strike. Transferred to the Law and Motion Calendar to be heard on the same date at 9:30 am in Dept. 302. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: DAVID KENT ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Petitioners Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 10, 2015, Line 3, Petitioners Motion To Vacate Arbitration Award. The petition to vacate is denied and the court confirms the arbitration award. The 8/15/14 letter does not clearly object to the timeliness of the final award. The parties agreed that the arbitrator would still decide the prevailing party issue and the gist of that letter is that the interim award is the final award. Petitioner has waived the issue per CCP 1283.8. Petitioner's other arguments also lack merit. The interim award was interim and an arbitrator is allowed to adjudicate the case in an incremental manner. The arbitrator had the authority to determine the prevailing party. Petitioner is basically arguing a legal error, which is not reviewable. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: MOHSIN AHMMED VS. BANK OF AMERICA N.A. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Bank Of America'S Motion For Protective Order Related To Notices Of Depositions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday March 10, 2015, Line 3, Defendant Bank Of America's Motion For Protective Order Related To Notices Of Depositions. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The motion for protective order is DENIED. However, each of the seven deposition notices is defective on its face as follows: Rohit Upadhya, Royce Allen, Amylee Casey, and William T. Booker are no longer employed by defendant, therefore subpoenas would be required to compel their appearance; Tammie Brown, Viola Floyd and Jonathan Murphy each reside outside the geographical limit for the place of deposition as set in the notices. The Court expresses no opinion as to the appropriateness of the document requests nor whether or not the identified witnesses are proper deponents in the case. The requests for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROY MILLER et al VS. MARCUS DANIEL MERCHASIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories General By Defendant William Hedden And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant William Hedden And His Attorney Steven F. Helfand Jointly And Severly; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities; Separate Statement; Declaration Of Rey Hassan And Exhibits
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Tuesday March 10, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF ROY MILLER, MARIO GALANDE, MILLER - GALANDE ANTIQUES' Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories General By Defendant William Hedden And For Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant William Hedden And His Attorney Steven F. Helfand Jointly And Severly. Pro Tem Judge Noah Lebowitz, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's motions to compel are DENIED. Notice of motions to compel further responses to interrogatories must be filed and served within 45-days of receipt of responses. CCP § 2030.300(c). These three motions were not served until four days after that 45-day jurisdictional limit expired. Plaintiff's request for sanctions is DENIED. Defendant's request for sanctions is GRANTED in the amount of $4,250.00, payable within 14 days. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to noah@dplolaw.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: O. C. et al VS. HOUSE OF AIR, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 10, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT HOUSE OF AIR, LLC DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. The demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to the first cause of action for gross negligence. California does not recognize such a cause of action and plaintiff can raise the issue in response to defendant's release/waiver defense without pleading the matter in the complaint. See City of Santa Barbara v. Sup. Crt. (2007) 41 Cal.4th 747, 779-780; Continental Ins. Co. v. American Protection Industries (1987) 197 Cal.App.3rd 322. The court overrules the demurrer to the negligent hiring, supervision, etc. cause of action. See CCP 452. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: ARKADY BERGER VS. GARY VARUM ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Quash Or Modify The Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records Directed To First Republic Bank, Gateway Fsb Bank, Marion Iles, Parkside Lending, Llc And United Commercial Bank
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 10, 2015, Line 14, DEFENDANT GARY VARUM, ALEX VARUM, CHARLES THIEL, NATALIE KREIGEL, 1122 UNIVERSITY, LLC, CALIFORNIA FAMILY COMPANY, LLC, CWR HOLDINGS, LLC, IRINA VARUM Motion To Quash Or Modify The Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records Directed To First Republic Bank, Gateway Fsb Bank, Marion Iles, Parkside Lending, LLC And United Commercial Bank. The parties have indicated in an email to the Court that they stipulate to Judge Pro Tem Chuck Geerhart's findings on the motion. Judge Goldsmith will execute an order that adopts Judge Pro Tem Geerhart's findings. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which sets forth Judge Pro Tem Geerhart's findings and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number.

rulings.Case Title: INS COMM STATE OF CA VS WESTERN EMPLOYER
rulings.Calendar Matter: Western Employers Insurance Companyâ¿¿S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Approval Of Reinsurance Commutation And Settlement Agreement With Onebeacon Insurance Company
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Tuesday, March 10, 2015, Line 13, RESPONDENT WESTERN EMPLOYERS INSURANCE COMPANY OF AMERICA Western Employers Insurance Company's Motion For Approval Of Reinsurance Commutation And Settlement Agreement With Onebeacon Insurance Company. Granted. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)
